tagged_story_for_tts:
Missing "a" or "the".<[silence:60ms]> Words that fill gaps.<[silence:60ms]>

The terms zero articles or null determiners describe when languages skip "a," "an," or "the" entirely—like Russian’s "я люблю яблоки" (I love apples), where no word stands in for "the." Other languages replace them.<[silence:60ms]> Japanese uses 助詞 (joshi) like "が" (ga) to mark subjects instead of articles:<[silence:80ms]> "ねこがいる" (neko ga iru—"a cat exists").<[silence:60ms]> Here, "が" signals the cat is new info;<[silence:80ms]> no separate word means "a."

Some languages swap articles for demonstratives (this/that).<[silence:60ms]> Arabic often drops "al-" (the) when context is clear, but adds "هَٰذَا" (hādhā—this) to specify:<[silence:80ms]> "هَٰذَا كِتَابٌ" (this [is] a book).<[silence:60ms]> No "a," just a pointer.<[silence:60ms]>

Even English does this with possessives:<[silence:80ms]> "She grabbed friend’s coat" (no "the" before friend’s).<[silence:60ms]> The ’s acts like an invisible article, locking the noun to its owner.<[silence:60ms]>

Three related subjects are grammar without articles, how context replaces words, and languages with no "the."
clean_story:
Missing "a" or "the". Words that fill gaps.

The terms zero articles or null determiners describe when languages skip "a," "an," or "the" entirely—like Russian’s "я люблю яблоки" (I love apples), where no word stands in for "the." Other languages replace them. Japanese uses 助詞 (joshi) like "が" (ga) to mark subjects instead of articles: "ねこがいる" (neko ga iru—"a cat exists"). Here, "が" signals the cat is new info; no separate word means "a."

Some languages swap articles for demonstratives (this/that). Arabic often drops "al-" (the) when context is clear, but adds "هَٰذَا" (hādhā—this) to specify: "هَٰذَا كِتَابٌ" (this [is] a book). No "a," just a pointer.

Even English does this with possessives: "She grabbed friend’s coat" (no "the" before friend’s). The ’s acts like an invisible article, locking the noun to its owner.

Three related subjects are grammar without articles, how context replaces words, and languages with no "the."
